diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Kostmann’s syndrome with malignant transformation into myelodysplastic syndrome (MDS) progressing to acute myeloid leukemia
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was based on the patient's history of severe congenital neutropenia, recurrent bacterial infections since birth, and bone marrow aspiration findings suggestive of myelodysplastic syndrome progressing to acute myeloid leukemia. The patient's clinical presentation included frequent pyogenic infections, abscesses in various parts of the body, and persistent neutropenia. Imaging studies confirmed the presence of abscesses and consolidation in the lungs. The patient was receiving G-CSF therapy, which is indicative of Kostmann’s syndrome.
